name: Robert Altman
abstract: Robert Bernard Altman ( AWLT-mən; February 20, 1925 – November 20, 2006) was an American filmmaker. He is considered an enduring figure from the New Hollywood era, known for directing subversive and satirical films with overlapping dialogue and ensemble casts.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: That was written... as a theme for, That Cold Day in the Park, which was the first movie I wrote with Robert Altman
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: We did all the music for the sound track, and I was the musical coordinator of that project.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I met Kathryn Altman, who was Robert Altman, the great director, his wife. She said, “I want to introduce you to my husband.” ... I met him. ... He suddenly said, “Annie.” ... He said, “My next project, if it flies, you got a part.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: Catherine Altman, who is married to Robert Altman, the great film maker, she’s one of my best friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: Robert Altman’s “Jazz 34,” which is the outtakes, the outtakes of Kansas City.
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012
